content.cn_text: 您将听到一场关于汽车对城市发展影响的讲座。
content.en_text: You will hear a lecture on the influence of the car on urban development.

content.cn_text: 首先，您将有一些时间阅读第31至40题。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 您
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答第31至40题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 大家早上好。
content.en_text: Good morning, everyone.

content.cn_text: 今天，在本系列讲座的第二讲中，我们将把注意力转向交通。
content.en_text: Today, in this second lecture of the series, we turn our attention to transportation.

content.cn_text: 我们将特别探讨汽车是如何影响澳大利亚城市发展的
content.en_text: In particular, we're going to take a look at the way the motor car influenced urban development in Australia

content.cn_text: 以及从20世纪40年代到90年代末，它如何改变了我们的生活方式。
content.en_text: and changed our lifestyle from the 1940s to the end of the 1990s.

content.cn_text: 如今汽车如此普遍，以至于我们都习以为常，几乎不曾思考它对我们社会产生的重大影响。
content.en_text: The car is so common nowadays that we all take it for granted and we barely think about the major effect it has had on our society.

content.cn_text: 但让我们暂时回到20世纪40年代末。
content.en_text: But let's go back for a while to the late 1940s.

content.cn_text: 尽管汽车自20世纪初就已出现，但那时在澳大利亚的主要城市，每八个人中只有一人拥有一辆汽车。
content.en_text: Although they had been around since the start of the 20th century, only one person in eight owned a car in Australia's major cities at that time.

content.cn_text: 当时，近60%的出行仍通过各种公共交通工具完成，
content.en_text: Nearly 60% of journeys were still made by various means of public transport,

content.cn_text: 而只有15%的出行是驾车完成的，这与现在的统计数据相比显然非常少。
content.en_text: and only 15% by car, which is clearly very few compared to statistics now.

content.cn_text: 在汽车普及之前，公交车非常拥挤，
content.en_text: Buses were very crowded in the days before extensive car use,

content.cn_text: 而且它们也被认为是不舒服的，尽管提供了一个大多数人负担得起的廉价选择。
content.en_text: and they were also considered to be uncomfortable despite providing a cheap option that most people could afford.

content.cn_text: 请记住，这距离二战结束没多久。
content.en_text: Remember, this wasn't long after the Second World War.

content.cn_text: 人们厌倦了物质匮乏、凑合着过的生活，对他们来说，汽车代表着自由。
content.en_text: People were tired of living without things, of making do, and to them cars represented freedom.

content.cn_text: 但即便对于那些少数拥有汽车的富裕人士来说，当时养车也不容易。
content.en_text: But even for the wealthy few who did have vehicles, it wasn't easy to run a car at this time.

content.cn_text: 除了购车的费用，零配件也难以获取，因为许多都是进口的。
content.en_text: As well as the expense of buying a car, spare parts were difficult to get, as many were imported.

content.cn_text: 车主面临的另一个问题是燃油仍然实行配给制，而且汽车税非常高。
content.en_text: Another problem for car owners was that fuel was still rationed, and car taxes were very high.

content.cn_text: 针对这些问题，来自各个压力团体的强烈公众情绪导致澳大利亚政府的更迭，
content.en_text: Strong public feeling on issues like these from various pressure groups resulted in a change of government in Australia

content.cn_text: 发生在1949年大选之后，这无疑塑造了我们国家的未来。
content.en_text: after the general election in 1949, which undoubtedly shaped the future of our nation.

content.cn_text: 在20世纪60年代，更便宜、更可靠的汽车变得普及，这与郊区的快速扩张同时发生。
content.en_text: In the 1960s, cheaper, more reliable cars became available, and this coincided with the rapid expansion of the suburbs.

content.cn_text: 汽车使得这些地区可以向各个方向发展，远远超出了19世纪铺设的火车线路。
content.en_text: The car enabled these to grow in all directions, far beyond the train lines laid down in the 19th century.

content.cn_text: 在过去的150年里，市中心一直是一个享有盛誉的区域，但汽车的增加使得主要街道变得拥堵。
content.en_text: For the previous 150 years, the city centre had been a prestigious area, but the addition of cars made the main streets congested.

content.cn_text: 这并非唯一的问题。现在它们也变得污染严重了。
content.en_text: This wasn't the only problem. Now they were polluted as well.

content.cn_text: 由于这些街道通常是直的，它们引诱驾车者超速。
content.en_text: Since these streets were usually straight, they tempted motorists to speed.

content.cn_text: 因此，新街道的布局受到了这种向私人交通转变的影响。
content.en_text: So the layout of newer streets was affected by this change towards private transport.

content.cn_text: 在新区域，道路不再是直的。相反，它们有专门设计的弯道，以迫使驾驶员减速。
content.en_text: In the newer areas, roads were no longer built straight. Instead, they had curves that were specially designed to force drivers to slow down.

content.cn_text: 这意味着它们更长了，但这并不是一个问题，因为人们很少再步行了。
content.en_text: This meant they were longer, but this was not a problem because people rarely walked anymore.

content.cn_text: 到了20世纪70年代，修建的道路类型发生了变化，每个方向都有更多的车道。
content.en_text: By the 1970s, the type of roads being built changed, with more lanes in each direction.

content.cn_text: 通勤者更多了，因为现在他们可以更快地到达工作的城市。
content.en_text: They were more commuters, because now there was faster access to the city where they worked.

content.cn_text: 此时，汽车正影响着生活的许多不同方面。
content.en_text: By this time, the motorcar was affecting many different areas of life.

content.cn_text: 它甚至，令人惊讶地，影响了人际关系。
content.en_text: It even, surprisingly, affected relationships.

content.cn_text: 在此之前，与任何居住在步行或骑自行车距离之外的人见面都是不切实际的，甚至是不可能的。汽车使人们聚在了一起。
content.en_text: Before this, meeting anyone who lived beyond walking or cycling distance was impractical, if not impossible. The car brought people together.

content.cn_text: 汽车还有哪些其他影响呢？
content.en_text: What other effects did the car have?

content.cn_text: 汽车使用的增加改变了我们广告宣传的方式。以前，广告设计是为了吸引缓慢移动的行人，
content.en_text: The increasing car use changed the way we advertise. Previously, advertisements had been designed to attract slow-moving pedestrians,

content.cn_text: 因此它们被放置在视线高度，并以人的尺度设计。
content.en_text: so they were located at eye level and designed on a human scale.

content.cn_text: 到了20世纪70年代末，大型广告被设计用来吸引过往驾车者的注意。
content.en_text: By the late 1970s, massive advertisements were designed to attract the notice of the passing motorist.

content.cn_text: 在20世纪80年代和90年代，甚至商店的位置也开始发生变化。
content.en_text: In the 1980s and 90s, even the location of shops began to change.

content.cn_text: 我们在郊区建造了购物中心，这意味着旧城区的生意大大减少了。
content.en_text: We built shopping malls out in the suburbs, which meant that old town centres did far less business.

content.cn_text: 接着，我们看到了免下车购物中心、剧院乃至餐厅的发展，这些如今确保我们尽可能少地下车。
content.en_text: Next, we saw the development of drive-in shopping centres, theatres and even restaurants, which now ensure we get out of our cars as little as possible.

content.cn_text: 现在，我想请大家关注一些与同期汽车拥有量相关的统计数据。
content.en_text: Now, I'd like to turn your attention to a few statistics related to car ownership over the same period.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。现在您有一分钟时间检查第四部分的答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of Part 4. You now have one minute to check your answers to Part 4.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS only for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31-40.

items.title: 31. __________ transport was used for 60% of journeys
Result of pressure from motorists – new 35. __________ in Australia in 1949
Main streets were congested and 36. __________ (and usually straight)
Car use affected the 38. __________ between people
New types of 39. __________ were designed to attract motorists
Less business in town centres because of introduction of 40. __________
